prevalence.source: Coloproctology epidemiology syntheses; primary care data
prevalence.trend: stable; higher with aging and sedentary lifestyle

burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; significant discomfort and QOL impact

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical evaluation with anorectal exam and anoscopy; rule out other causes of bleeding

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High for mild disease with lifestyle and topical therapy

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Primary care and coloproctology evaluation; anoscopy in specialty clinics
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Fiber, hydration, topical treatments; rubber band ligation for grade II/III; hemorrhoidectomy for severe cases
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Delayed care due to stigma and limited specialist access in some regions
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Effective low-cost treatments and surgical options available in major centers
